title: Top 25 Prompt Engineering Voices of 2026
subtitle: The most influential prompt engineers, AI engineers, and educators shaping how professionals work with AI
methodology: Ranked by cross-list appearances, community impact, published work, and relevance to prompt engineering and AI engineering. Sources include DAIR.AI, AI education platforms, LLM tooling communities, and independent analysis. We evaluated 50+ candidates and filtered for people who actively advance how professionals interact with AI systems.

voices.name: Riley Goodside
voices.title: Staff Prompt Engineer
voices.bio: The original prompt engineer. First person to hold the 'Staff Prompt Engineer' title at Scale AI. His early explorations of GPT-3 prompt injection, jailbreaking, and creative prompting techniques on Twitter defined the field before it had a name. Every prompt engineering technique traces some lineage back to his experiments.

voices.name: Simon Willison
voices.title: Creator & Developer
voices.bio: Creator of Datasette and the LLM command-line tool. Co-creator of Django. His blog is the most comprehensive technical resource on working with LLMs in practice. Covers prompt engineering, tool use, RAG, and AI safety with a depth and rigor that no one else matches. The technical conscience of the prompt engineering community.

voices.name: Elvis Saravia
voices.title: Founder
voices.bio: Created the Prompt Engineering Guide (promptingguide.ai), the most-starred and most-referenced prompt engineering resource on GitHub (50K+ stars). Founded DAIR.AI to democratize AI research and education. His guide is the curriculum that thousands of practitioners learned prompt engineering from.

voices.name: Lilian Weng
voices.title: Head of Safety Systems
voices.bio: Head of Safety Systems at OpenAI. Her blog (lilianweng.github.io) is the most technically rigorous resource on LLM agents, prompt engineering patterns, and AI systems design. Posts like 'Prompt Engineering' and 'LLM Powered Autonomous Agents' are canonical references cited in research papers and production systems alike.

voices.name: Andrej Karpathy
voices.title: Founder & Educator
voices.bio: Former Director of AI at Tesla and founding member of OpenAI. Now building Eureka Labs for AI education. His YouTube lectures on neural networks and LLMs have millions of views. His 'Let's build GPT from scratch' video is the single best resource for understanding how the models behind prompt engineering actually work.

voices.name: Ethan Mollick
voices.title: Professor & Author
voices.bio: Wharton professor and author of 'Co-Intelligence.' His Substack 'One Useful Thing' is where non-technical professionals learn how to prompt effectively. His research on AI's impact on knowledge work showed that prompting skill is the key differentiator in AI-augmented performance.

voices.name: Swyx (Shawn Wang)
voices.title: Founder
voices.bio: Runs the Latent Space podcast and newsletter covering AI engineering. Coined 'AI Engineer' as a role category. His content on building AI applications, prompt chains, and agent architectures bridges the gap between prompt engineering and software engineering.

voices.name: Harrison Chase
voices.title: CEO & Co-Founder
voices.bio: Built LangChain, the most popular framework for building LLM applications. While not a 'prompt engineer' by title, LangChain's abstractions for prompt templates, chains, and agents defined how the industry structures prompt-driven applications. The tooling that made prompt engineering scalable.

voices.name: Sander Schulhoff
voices.title: Researcher & Author
voices.bio: Created Learn Prompting (learnprompting.org), one of the most comprehensive free prompt engineering courses. Lead author of 'The Prompt Report,' a systematic review of prompting techniques across 1,500+ papers. His academic approach to cataloging prompt techniques brought rigor to a field that was mostly vibes.

voices.name: Jeremy Howard
voices.title: Co-Founder
voices.bio: Co-founded fast.ai (making deep learning accessible) and Answer.AI. Former President and Chief Scientist at Kaggle. His practical AI education philosophy shaped how an entire generation learned ML. Now focused on making AI tools work for everyday users through Answer.AI.

voices.name: Jason Liu
voices.title: Creator
voices.bio: Created Instructor, the most popular library for structured output from LLMs. His work on making LLMs return reliable, typed data instead of free text solved one of the biggest practical problems in prompt engineering. Active teacher on prompt design patterns.

voices.name: Rowan Cheung
voices.title: Creator
voices.bio: Forbes 30 Under 30 (2026). 2M+ newsletter subscribers. While broader than prompt engineering, his coverage of AI tools, techniques, and practical applications is how many professionals first discover prompt engineering concepts and stay current on what's possible.

voices.name: Allie K. Miller
voices.title: AI Entrepreneur & Advisor
voices.bio: 2M LinkedIn followers. Her AI-First Course has 350K+ students learning practical AI skills including prompt engineering. Named to Time's 100 Most Influential People in AI. Makes prompt engineering accessible to business professionals, not just engineers.

voices.name: Matt Shumer
voices.title: CEO
voices.bio: Built HyperWrite, one of the first AI writing assistants built on advanced prompting. His open-source prompt optimization tools and agent frameworks push the boundaries of what's possible with careful prompt design.

voices.name: Rome Thorndike
voices.title: Founder
voices.bio: Built PE Collective into a community of 1,300+ prompt engineers and AI practitioners. Runs AI News Digest newsletter with 3,000+ subscribers. Created the job board and salary data that gave the prompt engineering profession its first career intelligence platform.

voices.name: Chip Huyen
voices.title: Author & ML Engineer
voices.bio: Author of 'Designing Machine Learning Systems.' Her writing on building production AI systems, including prompt management, evaluation, and deployment, provides the systems-level thinking that prompt engineers need as they move from experimentation to production.

voices.name: Philipp Schmid
voices.title: Tech Lead
voices.bio: Tech lead at Hugging Face. His tutorials on fine-tuning, prompt engineering for open-source models, and deploying LLMs have become the standard reference for practitioners working outside the OpenAI ecosystem.

voices.name: Yolanda Gil
voices.title: Research Professor & VP
voices.bio: President of AAAI and research professor at USC. Her work on AI for science and knowledge capture provides the theoretical foundation for how prompt engineering connects human expertise with AI capabilities at an institutional level.

voices.name: Zain Kahn
voices.title: Founder
voices.bio: 1M LinkedIn followers. 1.5M+ newsletter subscribers. His practical prompt templates and AI workflow guides reach the largest audience of business professionals learning prompt engineering. Makes complex techniques simple enough that non-technical users can apply them immediately.

voices.name: Rachel Woods
voices.title: Founder
voices.bio: Former Meta data scientist. Built The AI Exchange into a community of 1,800+ paying members learning practical AI skills. Her courses on prompt engineering for business professionals bridge the gap between technical capabilities and real workflows.

voices.name: Hammad Bashir
voices.title: Co-Founder
voices.bio: Co-founded Chroma, the open-source vector database that powers RAG applications. While focused on infrastructure, his work on retrieval-augmented generation directly enables the most powerful prompt engineering pattern: grounding LLM responses in real data.

voices.name: Ruben Hassid
voices.title: AI Content Creator
voices.bio: 280K LinkedIn followers. Ranked 2nd World Content Creator on LinkedIn by Favikon. Specializes in actionable prompt templates for scaling content with AI. His practical, hands-on approach helps professionals implement prompt engineering in their daily workflows.

voices.name: Jeremy Nguyen
voices.title: AI Educator
voices.bio: AI educator at Monash University and one of the most practical voices on prompt engineering for business. His frameworks for structuring prompts, evaluating outputs, and building AI workflows help professionals go from 'playing with ChatGPT' to systematic prompt engineering.

voices.name: Dan Shipper
voices.title: CEO & Editor
voices.bio: Runs Every, an AI-native media company. His 'Chain of Thought' column documents how he uses prompting to build products, write, and think. One of the best examples of a professional who integrated prompt engineering deeply into their creative and business workflow.

voices.name: Cassie Kozyrkov
voices.title: CEO
voices.bio: Former Chief Decision Scientist at Google. 1M LinkedIn followers. Her content on AI literacy and decision-making with AI tools shapes how professionals think about when and how to use prompting vs. other approaches. The voice of pragmatism in a hype-heavy field.
